trackingsummarydata.subject: Subject line: This week in A&S: Don't miss convos with journalists, composers this week
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 03/02/26

urls.page_title: Culler Lecture in Critical Theory: "Technofascism" by Zahid R. Chaudhary - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Zahid R. Chaudhary, Associate Professor of English, Princeton University, will deliver this year's Culler Lecture in Critical Theory. Chaudhary, who received his Ph.D. from Cornell Unviersity, specializes in postcolonial studies, visual culture, a...
urls.page_title: Historical marker commemorates Toni Morrison’s time in Ithaca
urls.page_summary: Cornell faculty, staff, students and community members celebrated the 95th birthday of Toni Morrison, M.A. ’55, by unveiling a new historical marker in front of 513 N. Albany St., where she lived while in graduate school.
urls.page_title: Global Hubs researchers probe war, how to keep peace | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Two Cornell researchers collaborated with colleagues in the United Kingdom in an effort to understand the organized violence of modern war.

The LaFeber-Silbey Lecture in History
A World Without Law? Lessons from History and Literature, from Nuremberg to Pinochet and Beyond
Reception to Follow - A.D. White House
Philippe Sands
Biography
Philippe Sands KC is Professor of Law at U...
urls.page_title: 2026 Krieger Lecture in American Political Culture with Cathy Cohen - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
Beyond Affordability and Trump: Young Adults’ Expansive Visions of the State, Democracy, and Racial Solidarity
Cathy J. Cohen is the D. Gale Johnson Distinguished Service Professor at the University of Chicago. She formerly served in numerous adm...
urls.page_title: Literary critic Biodun Jeyifo dies at 80 | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Biodun “BJ” Jeyifo, a leading literary critic and cultural theorist known for his analysis of modernity and its attendant social and cultural crises, died Feb. 11 in Lagos, Nigeria. He was 80.
urls.page_title: Proteins organize for extracellular electron transfer | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Researchers discovered electron transfer in electroactive bacteria is mediated by CymA proteins’ ability to synchronize and form a biomolecular condensate in the cell’s inner membrane.
urls.page_title: Give to College of Arts & Sciences | Cornell Giving Day
urls.page_summary: I’m ready to support College of Arts & Sciences on March 12, 2026 during Cornell Giving Day. Learn more about College of Arts & Sciences and all the other areas participating in Cornell Giving Day.
urls.page_title: Daniel Gold, professor of Asian studies emeritus, dies at 78
urls.page_summary: Daniel Gold, a professor of Asian studies emeritus in the College of Arts and Sciences, died on Feb. 16 in Ithaca. He was 78.
urls.page_title: On Democracy, Conservatism and Journalism: A Conversation with Bret Stephens - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Seth Klarman ’79, CEO and portfolio manager of The Baupost Group and 2026 Hatfield Fellow, will be in conversation with Bret Stephens, opinion columnist for the New York Times and Zubrow Distinguished Visiting Journalist, to discuss a wide range o...
